01a_quotes.title: AI in the Pen: How Real-time AI Writing Guidance Shapes Online Reviews

study_design: To test our research hypotheses, we follow recent developments in the literature (Han et al., 2022; Burtch et al., 2024, Eichenbaum et al., 2024, Goldberg et al., 2024) and employ a differences-in-differences approach that compares observations from calendar year 2023 (the inception of Yelp's AI writing guidance) with the previous year 2022 in seven major cities in the United States: Atlanta, Chicago, Houston, Los Angeles, Minneapolis, New York, and San Francisco, representing a mix of geographic areas across the country.
To account for holiday surges in review activity, we focus on reviews generated from the second week of January to the third week of November in both years, excluding the holiday seasons.

We follow the prior literature on online reviews and conduct our analyses at the business (Deng et al., 2022; Jiang et al., 2023) and review levels (Khern-am-nuai et al., 2018; Deng et al., 2022), aiming to provide a holistic view of the impacts.

Yelp's introduction of AI writing guidance serves as a cross-sectional shock affecting all restaurant reviews on the platform; we, therefore, employ a panel difference estimator as outlined by Goldberg et al. (2024) to compare review outcomes from year 2023 to those from year 2022.

The panel difference estimator is similar to a differences-in-differences approach, relying on the assumption that review volumes and characteristics exhibit seasonal stability from one year to the next.
task: We examine Yelp's AI writing guidance adopted in April 2023, a novel human-AI collaboration in content creation.
Yelp's AI writing guidance is designed to reduce reviewers' writer's block by providing interactive prompts—specifically, "Food," "Service," and "Ambiance."

When drafting a review, users can see these three topics displayed above their text box. Each topic helps users track what they have covered and reminds them of additional aspects they may wish to address.
Once a topic is included in the review, it turns green with a checkmark in real time.

However, users are not required to cover all three topics, thus highlighting all three keywords, so that they can post their reviews. In other words, the list of keywords serves primarily as a reminder rather than a mandatory requirement.
population: Our study examines Yelp, one of the largest user-generated review platforms in the United States. As of 2023, Yelp had hosted almost 265.29 million reviews in the United States.
We cover reviews up to that date from seven major cities in the United States: Atlanta, Chicago, Houston, Los Angeles, Minneapolis, New York, and San Francisco.

To ensure our analysis captures meaningful review activity, we focus on restaurants that maintain an active presence on the platform. Many restaurants receive few or no reviews over extended periods of time, making it difficult to assess trends in customer engagement and review dynamics.
To mitigate this issue and ensure a consistent level of activity in our sample, we restrict our analysis to active restaurants with at least one review posted in 2022.
The final dataset includes 44,019 restaurants and 1,559,528 reviews.
arms: We use observations from 2023 as the "treatment" group and observations from one year prior as the "control" group.
𝑇𝑟𝑒𝑎𝑡𝑡 is a dummy indicating year 2023, and 𝑃𝑜𝑠𝑡𝑤 is a dummy indicating weeks after April 25th (for both years).
outcome_definitions: Weekly Review Volume: the number of reviews posted per week at the restaurant level
Weekly Review Length: the average number of characters per review, based only on English-language reviews
Weekly Review Helpfulness Votes: the number of helpfulness votes received per week at the restaurant level
Length: the number of characters per review
𝐹𝑜𝑜𝑑𝑠𝑐𝑜𝑟𝑒: the proportion of review content attributed to the food topic
𝑆𝑒𝑟𝑣𝑖𝑐𝑒𝑠𝑐𝑜𝑟𝑒: the proportion of review content attributed to the service topic
𝐴𝑚𝑏𝑖𝑎𝑛𝑐𝑒𝑠𝑐𝑜𝑟𝑒: the proportion of review content attributed to the ambiance topic
𝐹𝑜𝑜𝑑𝑙𝑒𝑛𝑔𝑡ℎ: the amount of text devoted to food topics
𝑆𝑒𝑟𝑣𝑖𝑐𝑒𝑙𝑒𝑛𝑔𝑡ℎ: the amount of text devoted to service topics
𝐴𝑚𝑏𝑖𝑎𝑛𝑐𝑒𝑙𝑒𝑛𝑔𝑡ℎ: the amount of text devoted to ambiance topics
𝑇𝑜𝑝𝑖𝑐𝐸𝑛𝑡𝑟𝑜𝑝𝑦: a measure of textual complexity using the Shannon index of the review's topic features
Gunning Fog Index: estimates the years of formal education needed to understand a text, based on sentence length and the proportion of complex words
SMOG Index: estimates the grade level of a text based on the frequency of polysyllabic words
Lexical Density: measures the proportion of content words (nouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs) relative to total words
statistical_results: Numerically, we estimate that reviews written with the AI writing guidance were on average about 4.8% longer, while the number of reviews decreased by roughly 2.5%.
Quantitatively, reviews posted on Yelp received, on average, 3.7% fewer helpfulness votes under AI writing guidance.

Results are reported in Table 2. Results reported in columns (1) – (3) indicate that the introduction of AI writing guidance significantly increased the likelihood that users discussed service, while simultaneously reducing the relative emphasis on food.
Columns (4) – (6) further reveal that the amount of text devoted to all topics increased substantially.
Table 3 reports the regression results, which indicate that the AI writing guidance significantly increased review length while decreasing the volume of reviews.

In Table C1, most coefficients are not statistically significant in pre-treatment periods. In particular, only six out of 70 coefficients (for the pretreatment dummies) are statistically sound at a significance level of 95% or higher.
The results in Appendix F remain consistent with our main analysis, confirming the robustness of our findings.

The estimation results, reported in Appendix I, align with our main analysis and support the robustness of our findings.
ambiguity_or_caveats: Our sample period excludes the launch of ChatGPT in November 2022. The availability of ChatGPT applies to all restaurant reviews in 2023 (our "treatment" group), both before and after the introduction of the AI writing guidance. The DID approach employed in this research will minimize the impacts of ChatGPT.

To further alleviate the concern, we conduct two robustness checks, in which we use Google reviews of the same set of restaurants in 2023 as the "control" group or incorporate time trends. All results are consistent with the main findings.
Our analysis focuses on the immediate, post-adoption phase, capturing user behaviors and textual outcomes soon after the AI writing guidance was introduced on Yelp.

Helpfulness votes represent the cumulative number of votes a review has received, making them a non-decreasing measure over time. However, our dataset does not include historical vote trends. Consequently, older reviews are likely to have more helpfulness votes than more recent ones, as they have had more time to accumulate votes.

Unlike Yelp, Google allows users to submit ratings without accompanying text, potentially leading to differences in textual characteristics between the two platforms. Therefore, for the cross-platform analysis conducted as a robustness check, we focus solely on review volume and helpfulness votes, excluding textual characteristics.
Although our evidence comes from online reviews, the mechanisms can arguably generalize to other AI-augmented authoring contexts where humans remain primary writers.

01a_quotes.notes: This is an empirical study examining the effects of Yelp's AI writing guidance feature introduced in April 2023. The paper uses a differences-in-differences design comparing 2023 (treatment year) with 2022 (control year) across seven major U.S. cities. The analysis is conducted at multiple levels: restaurant level (main analysis), review level, and review writer level. The study employs topic modeling (LDA) to measure topic coverage and complexity, and uses readability indices (Gunning Fog, SMOG, Lexical Density) to assess review quality. Heterogeneous effects are examined by user experience level (experienced vs. less experienced users, classified by Yelp Elite badges). Multiple robustness checks are conducted including relative-time models, cross-platform analysis with Google reviews, and analyses with/without detrending and city-specific time trends.

01_extraction.title: AI in the Pen: How Real-time AI Writing Guidance Shapes Online Reviews
01_extraction.study_design_description: The study employs a differences-in-differences approach comparing observations from calendar year 2023 (the inception of Yelp's AI writing guidance) with the previous year 2022 in seven major cities in the United States: Atlanta, Chicago, Houston, Los Angeles, Minneapolis, New York, and San Francisco. To account for holiday surges in review activity, the analysis focuses on reviews generated from the second week of January to the third week of November in both years, excluding the holiday seasons. Analyses are conducted at both the business and review levels. A panel difference estimator is employed to compare review outcomes from year 2023 to those from year 2022, relying on the assumption that review volumes and characteristics exhibit seasonal stability from one year to the next.
01_extraction.primary_task_description: Yelp's AI writing guidance, adopted in April 2023, is designed to reduce reviewers' writer's block by providing interactive prompts—specifically, "Food," "Service," and "Ambiance." When drafting a review, users can see these three topics displayed above their text box. Each topic helps users track what they have covered and reminds them of additional aspects they may wish to address. Once a topic is included in the review, it turns green with a checkmark in real time. However, users are not required to cover all three topics, so the list of keywords serves primarily as a reminder rather than a mandatory requirement.
01_extraction.population_description: The study examines Yelp, one of the largest user-generated review platforms in the United States. As of 2023, Yelp had hosted almost 265.29 million reviews in the United States. The analysis covers reviews from seven major cities: Atlanta, Chicago, Houston, Los Angeles, Minneapolis, New York, and San Francisco. To ensure the analysis captures meaningful review activity, the study restricts analysis to active restaurants with at least one review posted in 2022. The final dataset includes 44,019 restaurants and 1,559,528 reviews.

01_extraction.notes: This is a differences-in-differences study examining the effects of Yelp's AI writing guidance feature introduced in April 2023. The analysis compares 2023 (treatment year) with 2022 (control year) across seven major U.S. cities. The final dataset includes 44,019 restaurants and 1,559,528 reviews. Analyses are conducted at both the restaurant and review levels. Heterogeneous effects are examined by user experience level (less experienced vs. experienced users, classified by Yelp Elite badges). The study employs topic modeling (LDA) to measure topic coverage and complexity, and uses readability indices (Gunning Fog, SMOG, Lexical Density) to assess review quality. Multiple robustness checks are conducted including relative-time models, cross-platform analysis with Google reviews, and analyses with/without detrending and city-specific time trends. The authors note that ChatGPT became available in November 2022, affecting both treatment and control groups, but the DID approach minimizes this impact. Helpfulness votes are cumulative and non-decreasing, so older reviews tend to have more votes. Arm-level sample sizes (n) are not explicitly stated in the quotes; the total dataset size is 44,019 restaurants and 1,559,528 reviews.

02_method.rationale: The study explicitly employs a differences-in-differences approach comparing 2023 (after Yelp's AI writing guidance inception) with 2022 (before), using a panel difference estimator. This is a classic quasi-experimental identification strategy exploiting the temporal rollout of the AI feature.

per_outcome.operationalization: Number of characters per review, measured from submitted review text
Count of reviews posted per week at the restaurant level
Number of helpfulness votes received per week at the restaurant level, as voted by other Yelp users
Proportion of review content attributed to the food topic, measured via topic modeling or text classification
Proportion of review content attributed to the service topic, measured via topic modeling or text classification
Proportion of review content attributed to the ambiance topic, measured via topic modeling or text classification
Character count of text devoted to food topics, identified via topic classification
Character count of text devoted to service topics, identified via topic classification
Character count of text devoted to ambiance topics, identified via topic classification
Shannon index calculated from the distribution of review topic features, measuring textual complexity
Readability score based on sentence length and proportion of complex words, estimating years of education needed
Grade level estimate based on frequency of polysyllabic words in the review text
Proportion of content words (nouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs) relative to total words in review

04_onet.rationale: The participants are Yelp users writing restaurant reviews. The primary task is 'drafting a review' where users document their dining experiences by recording information about food, service, and ambiance. This is a clear case of documenting/recording information—users are entering, transcribing, recording, storing, or maintaining information in written form. While the AI provides writing prompts to reduce writer's block, the core work activity being performed by the 1.5+ million reviewers is the act of documenting their experiences, not a broader occupation like 'writer' or 'content creator.'

llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: This is a natural experiment with a single treatment arm (2023, after Yelp introduced AI writing guidance in April 2023) compared to a control period (2022, before AI guidance). The treatment arm represents the human+AI condition.
llm_extracted.notes: This is a difference-in-differences natural experiment studying Yelp's AI writing guidance introduction. The outcome (review length in characters) is a throughput/productivity measure where larger is better. The regression uses log(Lengths) as the dependent variable, so the coefficient 0.048 directly represents the log ratio. The paper reports this as '4.8% longer' which matches the coefficient. Additional heterogeneous effects are reported for less experienced users (coef=0.046, SE=0.004) and experienced users (coef=0.028, SE=0.005), but the primary overall effect is used here. Sample sizes are not reported in the arms data.
source_quotes: Numerically, we estimate that reviews written with the AI writing guidance were on average about 4.8% longer, while the number of reviews decreased by roughly 2.5%. Treat*Post 0.048*** (0.004) for log(Lengths) in Table 3, Column 1
For less experienced users, Treat*Post 0.046*** (0.004) for log(Lengths) in Table 5, Panel A, Column 1
For experienced users, Treat*Post 0.028*** (0.005) for log(Lengths) in Table 5, Panel B, Column 1

computed.notes: This is a difference-in-differences natural experiment studying Yelp's AI writing guidance introduction. The outcome (review length in characters) is a throughput/productivity measure where larger is better. The regression uses log(Lengths) as the dependent variable, so the coefficient 0.048 directly represents the log ratio. The paper reports this as '4.8% longer' which matches the coefficient. Additional heterogeneous effects are reported for less experienced users (coef=0.046, SE=0.004) and experienced users (coef=0.028, SE=0.005), but the primary overall effect is used here. Sample sizes are not reported in the arms data. | CI missing; variance not computable

llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: After re-reading the full paper, this remains a quasi-experimental difference-in-differences study comparing restaurant-level aggregated outcomes before (2022) and after (2023) Yelp introduced AI writing guidance. This is NOT a randomized controlled trial with human-only vs human+AI arms at the individual level. The 'treatment' is a time period (2023) vs 'control' time period (2022), not individual-level random assignment. The paper reports regression coefficients from DiD models with log-transformed outcomes (e.g., log(Helpfulness Votes)), not raw means, SDs, or sample sizes per arm. The sample consists of 46,190 restaurants and 1,559,528 reviews aggregated at the restaurant-week level (847,902 observations in main analysis, Table 1). No per-arm means, SDs, or individual-level sample sizes are provided. The reported statistics are DiD interaction coefficients (Treat*Post) with standard errors, not t-statistics, F-statistics, or standardized effect sizes. P-values are reported as inequalities (p < 0.001, p < 0.01, p < 0.05) throughout, not exact values. The study design does not permit computation of Hedges' g for a human-only vs human+AI comparison because there are no such arms—only time periods with all users exposed to the AI guidance after April 2023. This is a policy evaluation, not an experiment comparing AI vs no-AI conditions for the same outcome measure.
llm_extracted.notes: This is a difference-in-differences quasi-experimental study, not an RCT. The 'arms' are time periods (2022 vs 2023) at the restaurant level, not individual-level treatment assignments. The paper reports DiD regression coefficients (Treat*Post) with standard errors for log-transformed outcomes, not means/SDs for treatment vs control groups. Sample: 46,190 restaurants, 1,559,528 reviews, 847,902 restaurant-week observations (Table 1). No per-arm n, means, or SDs are provided. P-values are reported as inequalities (p < 0.001, p < 0.01, p < 0.05) throughout Tables 2-9 and Appendices, never as exact values. No t-statistics, F-statistics, or standardized effect sizes (Cohen's d, Hedges' g) are reported. The study evaluates a platform-wide policy change (AI writing guidance introduced April 2023), not a randomized comparison of AI vs no-AI conditions. Cannot compute Hedges' g from the available information. The design does not fit the schema's requirement for a human-only baseline arm and a human+AI arm with comparable outcome measures.
source_quotes: The final sample contains 46,190 restaurants and their 1,559,528 reviews. (page 6, Section 4.2)
Table 1 summary statistics: Weekly Review Volume mean 1.663, Weekly Review Length mean 441.815, Weekly Review Helpfulness Votes mean 1.377, Num. Obs. at restaurant level (page 21)
Table 6, Column 1: Treat*Post -0.037*** (0.004) for log(Helpfulness Votes), Num. Obs. 847,902 (page 29)
Table 7, Panel A (Less Experienced Users): Treat*Post -0.023*** (0.003) for log(Helpfulness Votes), Num. Obs. 683,776 (page 30)
Table 7, Panel B (Experienced Users): Treat*Post -0.072*** (0.007) for log(Helpfulness Votes), Num. Obs. 293,448 (page 30)
Note: Standard errors in parentheses are robust and clustered by restaurant. ∗ p < 0.1, ∗∗ p < 0.05, ∗∗∗ p < 0.01. (repeated in all tables)
We employ a differences-in-differences approach that compares observations from calendar year 2023 (the inception of Yelp's AI writing guidance) with the previous year 2022 (page 5, Section 5.1)
log Y_itw = β1*Treat_t * Post_w + β2*Treat_t + γ'X_itw + δ_i + λ_w + ε_itw (Equation 1, page 22)
